item.title: The Uplift: Ellie the elephant
item.description: Meet Big Ellie, the elephant mascot of the WNBA's New York Liberty. Plus more heartwarming news.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian baby in the West Bank: Health ministry
item.description: Sam Fahd Abu Haikal was killed Friday evening, and his parents were wounded, the Palestinian health ministry said.

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade another round of fire
item.description: It's unclear how close the U.S. and Iran are to striking a deal, as an Iranian official says "the ball is in Trump's court," and Israel-Hezbollah hostilities continue.

item.title: No bags, no watch parties: What to expect when Trump attends NBA Finals
item.description: As President Trump prepares to watch the New York Knicks take on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden, officials are planning for a heightened security posture, sources said.

item.title: SoFi Stadium workers authorize strike ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup in LA
item.description: Roughly 2,000 SoFi Stadium workers overwhelmingly authorized a strike a week before the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off in Los Angeles.

item.title: Graham Platner faces another controversy days ahead of Maine Senate primary
item.description: This week, the New York Times reported allegations of Platner's "unsettling" behavior toward women he dated, including one claim that he was physically abusive, which Platner denies.

item.title: Becerra advances in California governor's race, CBS News projects
item.description: Former U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, will advance to the November election in the California governor's race, CBS News projects. A second candidate in the race has not yet been projected to advance.

item.title: Colombia's Petro accuses Trump of intervening in his country's election
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro argues the U.S. has chosen to align against his government and back forces he identifies as complicit in the drug trade.

item.title: Actor James Handy allegedly killed by girlfriend's son at LA home
item.description: Michael Gledhill, 44, was arrested on suspicion of murder after he turned himself in following the fatal stabbing of Handy, the LAPD said.

item.title: Los Angeles police chase ends in fatal shooting of suspect on 405 Freeway
item.description: The freeways of Los Angeles saw two big police pursuits on Friday. The first chase ended when authorities reported that a robbery suspect was shot and killed on the busy 405 Freeway during morning rush hour. In the second incident, an alleged carjacker was taken down by a police K-9 following a meandering three-hour chase. Carter Evans has more.

item.title: Pratt's lead over Raman narrows in LA mayor's race
item.description: CBS News projects that incumbent Mayor Karen Bass will advance to the November election, while her opponents, Councilmember Nithya Raman and political newcomer Spencer Pratt, compete for the final spot.

item.title: California primary results for key races including governor, LA mayor
item.description: The races for governor and Los Angeles mayor are among the most closely watched contests yet to be decided in California's primary elections.

item.title: NASA reverses evacuation order for ISS after leak concerns
item.description: NASA has reversed an evacuation order on the International Space Station due to concerns over two small air leaks in the Russian section. Retired NASA astronaut Scott Parazynski joins CBS News with more.

item.title: World waits for U.S., Iran to come to an agreement
item.description: President Trump said the situation with Iran seems to be going well, despite Iran claiming negotiations are stuck at a deadlock. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

item.title: Previewing the 2026 Tonys as "The Lost Boys" and "Schmigadoon!" lead in nominations
item.description: "The Lost Boys" and "Schmigadoon!" lead Tony nominations with 12 nods each heading into Sunday's Tony Awards, which air on CBS. Zach Stweart, chief critic at the theater information and ticket website TheaterMania, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump policies that halted immigration process for many
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island has struck down a series of Trump administration policies that were delaying the immigration process for hundreds of thousands of applicants. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: Colombian President Gustavo Petro on tensions with U.S.
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro opened up about his country's tense relationship with the U.S. as he prepares to leave office. In a wide-ranging interview, Petro spoke to CBS News national correspondent Lilia Luciano.

item.title: Why a 55-mile trek is a mandatory rite of passage for freshmen at one high school
item.description: The five-day, 55-mile Appalachian Trail hike is a 53-year tradition for freshmen at St. Benedict's Preparatory School.

item.title: Trump visits Wisconsin as job growth reported in May
item.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics released the May jobs report on Friday, with numbers that were significantly higher than experts' projections. President Trump traveled to Wisconsin to meet with dairy farmers in an attempt to energize his supporters. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady and Nikole Killion have more.

item.title: Space station crew briefly moves to "safe haven" amid concerns over leaks
item.description: Out of an abundance of caution, NASA briefly directed five of the seven crew members aboard the International Space Station to wait inside the docked SpaceX Crew Dragon "Freedom" spacecraft.

item.title: Becerra overtakes Hilton in California governor's race as Steyer faces narrowing path
item.description: Xavier Becerra, Steve Hilton and Tom Steyer emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election as vote counting continues.

item.title: New Jersey school requires freshmen to go on 55-mile hike
item.description: For more than five decades, St. Benedict's Preparatory School in Newark, New Jersey, has required its freshmen to go on a mandatory five-day, 55-mile hike on the Appalachian Trail. Steve Hartman explains why in "On the Road."

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for June 7, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Democratic Rep. Jim Himes and Republican Rep. Don Bacon.

item.title: Employers added 172,000 jobs in May, surging past expectations
item.description: The labor market continues to show strength despite rising inflation and concerns about slowing economic growth.

item.title: Marine veteran held at gunpoint fights off his attackers
item.description: A Marine veteran was working on his truck in front of his home in Oxon Hill, Maryland, this week, when four teens tried to rob him at gunpoint. That is when his military training kicked in. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: 6/5: CBS Evening News
item.description: New allegations raised against Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner; CBS News hears from Wisconsin voters on the economy.

item.title: After deadly bus crash, investigators probe company's links to other carriers
item.description: The company that operated a bus involved in a deadly crash in Virginia last week has ties to a broader network of travel firms, including one shut down by regulators a decade ago, a CBS News investigation has found.

item.title: 6/5: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: The U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May and the unemployment rate stayed at 4.3%; Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner has mounting controversies.

item.title: Ned Jarrett, NASCAR Hall of Famer and CBS announcer, dies at 93
item.description: Ned Jarrett was inducted into the NASCAR Hall of Fame in 2011 after 50 career wins on the sport's top circuit.

item.title: Quiet pressures reshaping the world of diamonds
item.description: Are diamonds really forever? For over five centuries, Antwerp's diamond district has been the cornerstone of the global diamond trade, through which 80% of the world's rough diamonds have passed. Now, that legacy is under strain in ways the city — and the industry itself — has never seen. Chris Livesay reports.
